items.title: A difficult departure leaves Labor nervous about One Nation's surge

children.content: WA minister and Labor stalwart Paul Papalia's resignation for family reasons opens his southern Perth seat to a by-election at a time when disgruntled voters are increasingly turning to One Nation.

articles.title: By-election to follow minister's resignation amid One Nation surge

children.content: WA Labor stalwart and senior state cabinet minister Paul Papalia has announced he is quitting politics after nearly two decades, triggering a by-election in his southern Perth seat.

gallery.altText: A man in a suit speaking at a lecturn

thumbnail.altText: A man in a suit speaking at a lecturn

items.title: Discounts 'too little, too late' after slow start to WA's northern tourist season

children.content: The WA government says a discounted tours scheme will help revitalise the Kimberley's sluggish start to its annual tourism season but operators have questioned the voucher program.

image.altText: A girl stands holding a suitcase at the end of the groyne at Town Beach in Broome looking out to the ocean

items.title: Visa woes behind him, Bryce Cotton stars for Boomers on return to Perth

children.content: Six-time NBL MVP Bryce Cotton shines as he pulls on Australian colours for the first time in the Boomers' FIBA World Cup qualifiers.

image.altText: Bryce Cotton shoots against the Philippines in a FIBA World Cup qualifier, arms outstretched with the ball going for goal.

items.title: Does Alcoa's $8b acquisition leave WA's jarrah forests in the firing line?

children.content: Alcoa's multi-billion-dollar deal to buy South32's alumina operations could unlock swathes of Western Australia's native jarrah forests for bauxite mining, environmentalists say.

gallery.altText: Kingsley Dixon sits on a hill of orange brown rubble, in front of a horizon of mined land.
Protesters gathered with signs opposing Alcoa's forest mining operations.

thumbnail.altText: Kingsley Dixon sits on a hill of orange brown rubble, in front of a horizon of mined land.
Protesters gathered with signs opposing Alcoa's forest mining operations.

items.title: Swimmer who disappeared from Perth beach 13 years ago likely killed by shark

children.content: Martyn Tann was last seen at Mullaloo Beach in 2013, with a coroner telling an inquest into his disappearance that she believed he was dead, likely in a shark attack.

image.altText: A man with blonde dreadlocks hair sits by a swimming pool

items.title: Australia urged to sign treaty banning nuclear weapons

children.content: There are renewed calls for Australia to sign a treaty ensuring nuclear weapons can't ever be facilitated, stored or manufactured in Australia.

image.altText: An orange flash had been enveloped by the great uprush of water. Immense clouds of smoke, steam and spray burst into the air.

items.title: 'Disgusting' antisemitic graffiti sprayed on Anzac landmark

children.content: Swastikas and anti-Jewish abuse were discovered spray-painted on rocks and the historic Plantagenet Battery along Western Australia's south coast.

gallery.altText: 'Heil hitler' and a swastika sprayed on a wall of a historic bunker in Albany.

thumbnail.altText: 'Heil hitler' and a swastika sprayed on a wall of a historic bunker in Albany.

items.title: Declining vaccination rates behind whooping cough spike across WA's south

children.content: Whooping cough cases across WA's Great Southern have already surged to more than three times last year's numbers, with parents urged to get their children vaccinated.

gallery.altText: A woman holds up a needle next to a vial.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holds up a needle next to a vial.

items.title: Families told accused Bali bomber Hambali to face 202 life terms

children.content: US military prosecutors reveal new details about their case against the man accused of planning the 2002 Bali bombings, telling the loved ones of Australian victims his trial could finally begin next year.

image.altText: A bespectacled middle aged Indonesian man with a grey beard but no moustache wears a black and white top.

items.title: WiseTech's Richard White to step down as chair, but remain on board

children.content: Shares in WiseTech global are surging in early trade. Wall Street has closed at a record level as oil prices settle to where they were before the war in Iran. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Richard White seated at a wooden boardroom table with a tablet in front of him.

items.title: 'A lot of stigma': The 'taboo' cancer side effects we don't talk about

children.content: Shame and stigma mean gynaecological side effects from cancer treatment — affecting women's sexual, urinary and reproductive health — are under-reported and under-researched.

image.altText: A woman speaks on a podium, she has short curly hair and a serious expression.

items.title: 'Incredibly vulnerable' industry becomes new target for scammers

children.content: Amidst a growing number of people taking up gig work to cope with high living costs, scammers are stealing the earnings of "already struggling" delivery drivers.

image.altText: A caucasian man wearing a dark green tshirt stands in front of a white car posing with a serious look

items.title: 'Not about race': Winmar statue removed two days after assault conviction

children.content: A statue immortalising a key moment in AFL history against racism is taken down, after its subject Nicky Winmar is found guilty of assaulting a woman.

items.title: Alex de Minaur suffers another 'truly gut-wrenching' Wimbledon loss

children.content: Alex de Minaur loses in the fourth round at Wimbledon in another match he feels he could, and maybe should, have won against ninth seed Flavio Cobolli.

image.altText: Alex de Minaur looks miserable at Wimbledon.

items.title: What you need to know before claiming your phone as a tax deduction

children.content: The cost of buying and using your own phone may be tax deductible, but the ATO has specific rules about how employees calculate these expenses.

items.title: Celebrate NAIDOC Week with these free films and TV series

children.content: As part of NAIDOC Week, we meet the Indigenous creatives who are re-shaping Australia's video game industry.

items.title: International experts weigh in on Australia’s bird flu response

children.content: While the H5N1 bird flu strain is relatively new to Australia, experts who have monitored the spread in other continents say surveillance is key to tackling the deadly virus.

items.title: 'Disappointing': Prince Harry and king's awkward stand-off over palace

children.content: The strained relationship between Prince Harry and his father, King Charles III, has been laid bare once again, after an awkward stand-off over accommodation at Buckingham Palace.

items.title: WorkSafe investigates after footballer suffers life-ending head injuries

children.content: Victoria's workplace safety watchdog is investigating after a suburban footy player was injured, and later died, after his head collided with a cricket pitch.

items.title: Rare barking geckos and Bungle Bungle sliders face extinction

children.content: New research has revealed the Pilbara barking gecko and Bungle Bungle robust slider are facing a heightened risk of extinction due to a rapidly warming climate.
